Making Alternatives Program (MAP) Program – Term 4 (10 weeks): is designed for young adults, aged 15-18 years seeking an alternative learning environment to mainstream schooling.  Students will participate in Career and Industry exploration activities beyond the classroom.  The program’s small-group setting will also provide for a more supportive and individualized approach to learning.

Springboard Program:  The Springboard Program provides outreach case management and funding to young people who are, or have been, in residential out-of-home care, between 16 and 21 years of age, who are disengaged from education, training or employment.  The young person has to be subject to a Custody of Secretary Order or a Guardianship Order on or after their 16th birthday to be eligible for Springboard support.

infringements that are related to homelessness, tenancy, credit and debt, guardianship and administration.
